entries.title: Homily on Acts 55 on acts 28:23
entries.excerpt: See again how not by miracles but by Law and Prophets he puts them to silence, and how we always find him doing this. And yet he might also have wrought signs; but then it would no longer have been matter of faith. In fact, this itself was a great sign, his discoursing from the Law and the Prophets.

entries.title: Homily on Acts 55 on acts 28:23
entries.excerpt: "And when they had appointed him a day, there came many to him into his lodging; to whom he expounded and testified the kingdom of God, persuading them concerning Jesus, both out of the Law of Moses and out of the Prophets, from morning till evening."

entries.title: Homily on Acts 55 on acts 28:23
entries.excerpt: Then he did not immediately answer, but gave them a day, and they came to him, and he discoursed, it says, "both out of the Law of Moses, and out of the Prophets."

entries.title: Commentary on Acts on acts 28:23
entries.excerpt: The Jews call faith in Christ a heresy. But even they testify that Christ is preached everywhere, although not all accept this preaching, and some of the Jews and Greeks even oppose it.
